resources.string: Build a concept quickly with reusable blocks, live preview, editable layers, and a portable JSON spec. No backend involved.
Swipe up for palette, templates, and recent blocks.
Search or pick from focused groups.
Expand a group, long-press an object, drag it onto the phone, and drop it where the screen should place it.
Drag blocks in from the palette. Drag existing blocks freely; long-press a block to show resize markers.
Tap a layer to edit it. Position and size are controlled directly on the phone preview.
Drag the selected block directly on the phone preview. Long-press it to show resize markers.
Choose a name for this design. It will be stored in the app private files on this device.
A saved project with this name already exists. Replace it?
Expand a group, long-press an object, and drop it on Live Preview.
UI Forge now works like a canvas-first builder. Use the bottom tabs to switch between Build, Layers, Inspect, and Export, then work directly on the phone preview.
In Build mode, tap Open to expand Add object. UI Objects are grouped by purpose. Expand a group, long-press an object, then drag it onto the phone canvas.
Templates are inside the Add object sheet. Choose a real-life starting point or tap New for a blank canvas.
The grid area is the design stage. Drop UI Objects anywhere inside the phone preview, drag existing objects freely, or long-press an object to show resize handles.
Use the Layers tab when the canvas is crowded. Tap a layer to select it and open Inspector, or delete the selected layer from the layer actions.
Use the Inspect tab or tap a widget to open Inspector. Edit content, layout, size, radius, spacing, opacity, alignment, style, and colors. Color fields show swatches and include Custom color.
Build -> Open -> drag an object to the phone -> tap it -> Inspect -> Save or Export.
The header shows UI Forge, project name, current mode, and the menu. Save stores locally, Load opens local projects, Preview returns to the canvas, and XML opens export.
Use the Build tab to open Add object. Templates are inside that sheet. Pick a template, tap New for a blank design, or long-press any UI Object and drag it onto the phone.
The white phone is the app screen. Drop objects anywhere, drag existing objects to move them, and long-press an object to show resize handles. Side handles resize one axis; corners resize both.
Tap an object or use Inspect to edit text, layout, dimensions, opacity, alignment, style, and colors. Use Layers when objects overlap or are hard to select on the canvas.
Export copies or shares the design spec. The header menu opens About. If the app fails, use Read logs there or send Downloads/uidesignerFailLog.txt.

1. Header: the top area shows UI Forge, the current project name, the active mode, and the menu button. The project name is the name used when saving.

2. Action strip: Save stores the current project locally. Load opens saved local projects. Preview scrolls back to the design stage. XML opens the export flow for the current design spec.

3. Build tab: shows the compact Add components card. Tap Open to expand the Add object sheet.

4. Add object: UI Objects are grouped by purpose. Expand a group, long-press an object, drag it to the phone preview, and drop it anywhere on the canvas.

5. Templates: templates are inside Add object. Choose a real-life starting point or tap New for a blank design.

6. Canvas: the grid panel is the working area and the white phone is the app screen. Placement is free, not locked to rows.

7. Move: drag an existing object directly on the phone preview.

8. Resize: long-press an object to show resize handles. Drag side handles for width or height, or corner handles for both.

9. Inspect: tap an object or use the Inspect tab to edit it. Use the close button in Inspector to return to Build.

10. Inspector controls: edit title, supporting text, width, height, text size, padding, corner radius, opacity, alignment, full-width mode, emphasized style, background color, and accent color.

11. Colors: color fields show swatches. Pick an existing color or use Custom color for RGB sliders and #RRGGBB hex input.

12. Layers: use the Layers tab when objects overlap or the canvas is crowded. Selecting a layer also opens Inspector.

13. Export: use the bottom Export tab or XML button to copy/share the design spec.

14. About and logs: tap the header menu for About. Use Read logs there if the app fails, or send Downloads/uidesignerFailLog.txt.
No log file found yet. When the app writes logs, they appear in Downloads/%1$s.
Could not read the log file
